name: paused-epic-file-refs-go-stale
body:
When resuming a long-paused epic/task, don't trust file paths named in its body — verify them first (grep/Read), don't just act on them.

Why: epic c0f3037f (prompt-cache) was paused ~1 week, its body cited `hooks/memory_loader_lc.py` and `hooks/dispatcher.py` as the UPS pipeline location. By the time it resumed, the pipeline had been refactored into `langchain_learning/session_graph.py` — the old file survived only as a stale .pyc. Acting on the stale path directly would have wired the cache-check node into dead code.

How to apply: before implementing a task whose body names specific files/functions and which has sat open for more than a few days, spend one Explore-agent pass confirming those paths still exist and still do what the body claims, before writing code against them. Applies especially to epics/tasks resumed via /task-grooming or /what-am-i-working-on.
